title: Add 3 critical missing enforcement gates as validation scripts
description: Implement 3 highest-impact enforcement gates as mechanical validation. Harness engineering > context engineering.

scope.in: Extend validate-project-json.sh with 2 new checks (project.json update verification, browser test verification). Add code-review failure blocking logic in 09-execute-projects.md.
scope.out: Creating new scripts. Adding the 2 deferred medium-priority gates. Changing existing validation behavior.

dod.criterion: validate-project-json.sh checks that completed tasks have DOD met fields set
validate-project-json.sh checks browser_test flag and warns if no design-review.md exists
09-execute-projects.md has explicit code-review failure blocking with max 1 fix cycle
Each validation script exits non-zero when condition is violated

tasks.title: Add project.json update and browser test checks to validate-project-json.sh
tasks.user_scenario: Pipeline stops with clear error if tasks are marked complete but DOD not verified, or if browser_test is true but no design review was logged.
tasks.scope: Extend validate-project-json.sh with: (1) For completed tasks, check that dod[].met is set (not all false). (2) If project has browser_test: true, check that a design-review.md exists in the project directory. Both checks should produce clear error messages. Script must exit non-zero on failure.

dod.criterion: Script detects completed tasks with all DOD met=false and reports error
Script detects browser_test=true without design-review.md and reports warning
Script exits non-zero on validation failure
Existing validation checks still pass for valid project.json files

tasks.title: Add code-review failure blocking logic in 09-execute-projects.md
tasks.user_scenario: When a code review returns 'fail', the pipeline stops and re-runs the builder before proceeding — bugs don't slip through to final review.
tasks.scope: Add explicit code-review failure handling in 09-execute-projects.md after the code-review phase section. If code_review_outcome = 'fail' or 'critical': (1) stop — do not proceed to review, (2) re-spawn builder with findings (bugs_found, data_flow_issues), (3) re-run code-review on updated diff, (4) max 1 fix cycle — if still failing, log and proceed to review. If 'pass': proceed normally.

dod.criterion: 09-execute-projects.md has code-review failure handling section with clear blocking logic
Max 1 fix cycle is specified to prevent infinite loops
Both 'fail' and 'critical' outcomes trigger the blocking logic
